title: Fermented foods like sauerkraut may boost gut defense
summary: Sauerkraut strengthens the gut barrier and reduces damage from inflammation. Fermentation produces helpful molecules.

title: Research reveals multiple barriers to reducing inhaler prescriptions to protect environment
summary: Researchers from Keele University have said there are multiple barriers preventing clinicians from prescribing alternative treatments for asthma patients, despite guidance calling for a move toward inhalers with lower environmental impact.

title: 15% of US cancer patients are active smokers at time of diagnosis, study finds
summary: Nearly half of patients diagnosed with cancer in 2023 had a history of smoking, and 15% were smokers at the time of their diagnosis, according to new Yale Cancer Center-led research published in JAMA Oncology.

title: Follow the Science: Why CD4 Testing is Critical in the Fight Against HIV/AIDS
summary: To meet ambitious targets for ending the HIV/AIDS epidemic, governments must unite in a global effort to improve access to CD4 testing.

title: Researchers uncover molecular connection between body fat and anxiety
summary: Researchers have revealed a link between body fat (adipose tissue) and anxiety, shedding light on the relationship between metabolism and mental health.

title: Doctor says these 7 subtle symptoms could be signs of cancer in children, parents watch out!
summary: 7 early warning signs of pediatric cancer: Here's what every parent should know | Health

title: Colon cancer is ‘becoming younger.’ Here’s how Stockton is raising awareness
summary: The third annual Crush Colon Cancer run/walk brought together about 200 people in front of City Hall in Stockton.

title: Advancing understanding and treatment of acute kidney injury
summary: Understanding and categorizing the various types of tubulointerstitial responses in acute kidney injury (AKI) may lead to the development of new treatments for the condition, according to Yale School of Medicine researchers who published a review in the Journal of Clinical Investigation on March 17.

title: Heart valve abnormality raises long term risk of dangerous arrhythmias
summary: People with a certain heart valve abnormality are at increased risk of severe heart rhythm disorders, even after successful valve surgery.

title: Investigating the SAINT iTBS Protocol for Bipolar I Depression
summary: The Stanford Accelerated Intelligent Neuromodulation Therapy (SAINT) for treatment-resistant major depressive disorder has now demonstrated promise for reducing depressive symptoms of bipolar I disorder in an open-label feasibility and safety trial.
